summary: Split Kirby's SpecialHi/Up-B code out of `ftkirbyspecialn.c` into a new matching object `ftkirbyspecialhi.c`, added its header, wired it into splits and configure.py, and adjusted includes/callback declarations so the new object links cleanly. The bot reported no GALE01 changes, indicating the split preserved the final binary.

key_files.role: Added a new split range for `melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftkirbyspecialhi.c` at text 0x800F21E8-0x800F331C and sdata2 0x804D9370-0x804D9388; shifted `ftkirbyspecialn.c` to start at 0x800F331C.
Registered `melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftkirbyspecialhi.c` as `Object(Matching, ...)` in the Kirby object list.
New 552-line source file containing Kirby SpecialHi entry, anim, IASA, physics, collision, projectile/accessory callback, and related helper code formerly embedded in `ftkirbyspecialn.c`.
New header declaring the public `ftKb_SpecialHi*`, `ftKb_SpecialAirHi*`, and `ftKb_AttackDashAir_800F22D4` functions for the split object.
Removed the SpecialHi code and unnecessary includes after the split; retained SpecialN-specific content.
Removed SpecialHi declarations from the broad Kirby header so they live in `ftkirbyspecialhi.h`.
Included `ftkirbyspecialhi.h` so Kirby code has access to the split declarations.
Moved/shared `ftKb_SpecialN_set_cbs2` as a Kirby inline helper used by the split code.
Included `ftKirby/ftkirbyspecialhi.h` for action-state/function table linkage and removed many no-longer-needed includes.
Cleaned now-unneeded includes as part of dependency tightening.

what_changed: Created `src/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftkirbyspecialhi.c` and moved the 0x800F21E8-0x800F331C Kirby SpecialHi code into it.

Created `src/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftkirbyspecialhi.h` with address-commented declarations for `ftKb_SpecialHi_Enter`, `ftKb_SpecialAirHi_Enter`, `ftKb_SpecialHi*_Anim`, `ftKb_SpecialAirHi*_Anim`, IASA, Phys, and Coll handlers.
Updated `config/GALE01/splits.txt` so `ftkirbyspecialhi.c` owns `.text` 0x800F21E8-0x800F331C and `.sdata2` 0x804D9370-0x804D9388, while `ftkirbyspecialn.c` now begins at 0x800F331C.
Removed SpecialHi declarations from `ftkirby.h` and included the new header from `ftkirby.c` and `ftdata.c`.
Moved/shared `ftKb_SpecialN_set_cbs2` into `ftKirby/inlines.h`, allowing the split file to reuse the callback helper without keeping it local to `ftkirbyspecialn.c`.
Reduced stale include dependencies in `ftkirbyspecialn.c`, `ftkirbyyoshiegg.c`, and `ftdata.c` after moving the code.
smart_moves: The split was backed by precise GALE01 split boundaries for both `.text` and `.sdata2`, avoiding accidental ownership overlap between `ftkirbyspecialhi.c` and `ftkirbyspecialn.c`.
The new object was immediately marked `Matching` in `configure.py`, making the split an actual linked matching unit rather than just a source organization change.
Public declarations were moved from the monolithic `ftkirby.h` into a focused `ftkirbyspecialhi.h`, reducing header sprawl and making ftdata/action-state dependencies explicit.
Keeping address comments on function prototypes preserved the address-to-symbol map during the move.
The PR preserved known matching hacks such as `PAD_STACK`, fake-match comments, and comma-operator regalloc tricks during the file move instead of trying to beautify code and risk breaking matching.
The build/link dependency cleanup in `ftdata.c` removed a large number of unrelated fighter/common includes while adding the one new Kirby SpecialHi header actually needed.
decomp_lessons: When extracting a subrange from a larger nonmatching or mixed source file, update both `splits.txt` and `configure.py` in the same PR so the linker layout and build object list stay synchronized.
For fighter action-state handlers, a dedicated move header is preferable to accumulating all move prototypes in the character root header.

Do not remove ugly-looking stack padding, fake conditionals, or comma operators during a split unless you are also proving the match; in this PR those constructs were preserved because they were part of the matching recipe.
Shared static helpers that become needed by multiple split files can be promoted into the character `inlines.h` rather than duplicated or left trapped in the original source file.

Include cleanup is safest after a split when guided by actual symbol use: `ftkirbyspecialn.c` dropped SpecialHi-only includes such as cliff/common/item cutter beam dependencies, while the new SpecialHi file gained them.
A bot report of `No changes` for GALE01 is strong evidence that a split/link PR preserved the binary, even when many source lines moved.
naming_conventions: New move-specific files follow lowercase character/move naming: `ftkirbyspecialhi.c` and `ftkirbyspecialhi.h`.
Kirby move symbols use the `ftKb_` prefix.
Ground and air variants are distinguished as `SpecialHi` versus `SpecialAirHi`.
Action-state callbacks use suffixes by phase: `_Enter`, `_Anim`, `_IASA`, `_Phys`, and `_Coll`.
Numbered phases are encoded in names such as `ftKb_SpecialHi1_Anim`, `ftKb_SpecialHi2_Phys`, and `ftKb_SpecialAirHiEnd_Coll`.
Address comments in prototypes use the existing short form such as `/* 0F2300 */`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Preserved `PAD_STACK(16)`, `PAD_STACK(4)`, `PAD_STACK(5)`, and `PAD_STACK(6)` padding in the moved SpecialHi routines.
Kept a fake-match conditional in `fn_800F21E8`: `if (!da->specialhi_projectile_spawn_x) { }`, annotated as produced via permuter.
Kept a comma-operator fake match in `ftKb_SpecialAirHi3_Phys` for register allocation: `((0, fp->co_attrs.air_drift_stick_mul))`.
Maintained helper temporaries such as `new_var`, explicit float constants, and re-fetching `GET_FIGHTER(gobj)` patterns where they were part of the matching source.
Used exact split addresses to carve out a matching object without changing the final GALE01 output.

review_feedback.interpretation: The binary comparison accepted the split/link change.

review_feedback.feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
review_feedback.interpretation: There is no evidence of reviewer-requested naming or structural changes beyond the submitted diff.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that remove fake-match constructs or `PAD_STACK` from `ftkirbyspecialhi.c`.
Search for other Kirby move split PRs that moved declarations out of `ftkirby.h` into move-specific headers.
Check whether `ftkirbyspecialn.c` became matching after the SpecialHi range was split out.
Look for follow-up cleanup around `ftKb_SpecialN_set_cbs2` in `ftKirby/inlines.h`.
Compare this split pattern with other fighter move files added as `Object(Matching)` in `configure.py`.
